abstract.para: Go is an open source, statically typed, compiled programming language designed for building reliable and efficient software. Go follows the agile lifecycle category.

section.title: Lifecycle and support policy
section.para: On &suselinux; 16, Go and its components are updated and supported according to the following rules:

listitem.para: Release cycle: Releases are kept in sync with upstream, which nominally occur every 6 months in February and August.
Version support: SUSE maintains versions N and N-1, mirroring the upstream support policy.
Minor releases: Go minor releases are continuously made available shortly after the upstream release.
Parallel installation: Different versions are parallel installable and undergo automated testing. The go metapackage always points to the latest go1.x package.
Support phases: Go is supported across the General Support and LTSS phases for all relevant &suselinux; minor releases.

section.title: Building Go applications
section.para: When building applications, developers have two options regarding versions:

listitem.para: To build with the latest recommended version, Go applications may depend on BuildRequires: golang(API) >= go1.23.
To control the timing of build changes, applications may pin to a specific go1.x package using BuildRequires: golang(API) == go1.23.

section.para: In both cases, you must use the major version of go1.x specified in the Go application's go.mod file, as the Go compiler now requires this as a minimum version.

section.title: FIPS compliance
section.para: Alongside standard go1.xx releases, &suse; provides a parallel go1.xx-openssl variant. This version is specifically patched to utilize OpenSSL for FIPS-compliant cryptographic operations.

Starting with go1.24, Go upstream introduced an integrated FIPS 140-3 cryptographic implementation. As this implementation achieves certification, the parallel go1.xx-openssl variant will be deprecated in favor of the upstream-supported FIPS mechanism. &suse; packaging will follow the upstream deprecation schedule and support lifecycle.
